LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - DEFENDANT EMBASIORA TEAME'S "MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE RELATED MATTERS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1048(A)" IS GRANTED. CIVIL CASE NOS. 579990 AND 584950 INVOLVE THE SAME TRAFFIC ACCIDENT WITH COMMON DEFENDANTS, SO IT WOULD BE EFFICIENT TO CONSOLIDATE THEM FOR ALL PURPOSES. PLAINTIFFS LEOPOLDO TORRES SANCHEZ AND JOSE NAVARRO-DIAZ OPPOSE, ARGUING THAT THE CASES ARE SO COMPLEX THEY WOULD CONFUSE A JURY IF CONSOLIDATED. HOWEVER, STATISTICS SHOW THAT SAN FRANCISCO JURIES ARE THE MOST EDUCATED IN THE NATION; THEY WOULD HAVE NO TROUBLE WITH THIS TRAFFIC ACCIDENT. THE FIRST-FILED CASE - 579990 - IS DESIGNATED THE LEAD. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2021 Continued To Mar-10-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion To Consolidate Related Matters Pursuant To Ccop Section 1048(A)
Off Calendar Jan-22-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Embasiora Teame's "motion to consolidate related matters pursuant to CCP section 1048(a)" is granted. Civil Case Nos. 579990 and 584950 involve the same traffic accident with common defendants, so it would be efficient to consolidate them for all purposes. Plaintiffs Leopoldo Torres Sanchez and Jose Navarro-Diaz oppose, arguing that the cases are so complex they would confuse a jury if consolidated. However, statistics show that San Francisco juries are the most educated in the nation; they would have no trouble with this traffic accident. The first-filed case - 579990 - is designated the lead.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Continued To Feb-03-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
